summary.text: Lake Marion, SC has about 110,600 surface acres, with a maximum depth near 76.77 feet. Confirmed sport fish include Largemouth Bass, Bluegill, Redear Sunfish Shellcracker, Black Crappie, White Crappie, White Perch, Channel Catfish, Blue Catfish, Flathead Catfish, Chain Pickerel. Habitat facts currently confirm 16 mapped fish attractors; mapped structure includes points, creekArms, channelLedges, humps.

regulations.reason: validated: state limits + lake exceptions (17 official sources)

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion has a surface area of 110,600 acres.
_extractedFacts.quote: Lake Marion, the largest reservoir in South Carolina, covers a surface area of 110,600 acres

_extractedFacts.fact: The maximum depth of Lake Marion is approximately 76.77 feet.
_extractedFacts.quote: a maximum depth of approximately 76.77 feet

_extractedFacts.fact: The average depth of Lake Marion is 13.12 feet.
_extractedFacts.quote: an average depth of 13.12 feet

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion is classified as having eutrophic conditions.
_extractedFacts.quote: The lake's ecosystem, characterized by eutrophic conditions with nutrient-rich waters

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion is owned and managed by Santee Cooper.
_extractedFacts.quote: Oversight of these operations is shared between Santee Cooper, the primary manager responsible for daily flow regulation and power production

_extractedFacts.fact: The primary dam forming Lake Marion is the Santee Dam, completed in 1941.
_extractedFacts.quote: The primary structure forming Lake Marion is the Santee Dam, an earthfill embankment completed in 1941

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion was formed in 1941 by the impoundment of the Santee River.
_extractedFacts.quote: Formed in 1941 by the impoundment of the Santee River

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion is part of the Santee River watershed.
_extractedFacts.quote: it impounds the upper reaches of the Santee River

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion has 511 miles of shoreline.
_extractedFacts.quote: a total shoreline length of 511 miles.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion has 16 public boat ramps.
_extractedFacts.quote: with 16 public boat ramps and multiple marinas.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion has a daily limit of five largemouth or spotted bass, each at least 14 inches.
_extractedFacts.quote: Lake Marion-specific rules including five largemouth or spotted bass daily, each at least 14 inches

_extractedFacts.fact: Striped bass fishing on Lake Marion is limited to a season from October 1 to June 15 with a three-fish limit.
_extractedFacts.quote: for striped bass, a season from October 1 to June 15 with a three-fish limit (fish 23-25 inches, one may exceed 26 inches).

_extractedFacts.fact: All watercraft and fishing are prohibited from November 1 through February 28 on Cantey Bay, Black Bottom, and Savannah Branch in Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.quote: All watercraft and fishing are prohibited Nov. 1 through Feb. 28 on Cantey Bay, Black Bottom and Savannah Branch in Lake Marion.

_extractedFacts.fact: There is a limit of no more than one blue catfish over 36 inches per person per day for Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.quote: No more than one blue catfish over 36 inches can be taken from Lakes Marion and Moultrie or the upper reaches of the Santee, Congaree and Wateree Rivers per person per day.

_extractedFacts.fact: Trotlines, traps, or eel pots are prohibited within 200 yards of any permanent man-made structure on Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.quote: No trotlines, traps or eel pots shall be placed within 200 yards of any permanent man-made structure on Lakes Marion and Moultrie

_extractedFacts.fact: Trotlines, traps, or eel pots are prohibited in the Diversion Canal connecting Lake Marion and Lake Moultrie.
_extractedFacts.quote: nor placed anywhere in the Diversion Canal connecting Lakes Marion and Moultrie

_extractedFacts.fact: Hooks used in Lake Marion must measure 7/16 inch or less between the point and shank.
_extractedFacts.quote: Hooks used in Lakes Marion and Moultrie ONLY must measure 7/16 inch or less between point and shank.

_extractedFacts.fact: There is a minimum size limit of 14 inches for largemouth bass in Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.quote: Lakes Blalock, Greenwood, Jocassee, Marion, Monticello, Moultrie, Murray, Secession, Wateree, Wylie, and the middle reach of the Saluda River and the upper reach of the Santee River—no largemouth bass less than 14 inches in total length.

_extractedFacts.fact: Recreational anglers are limited to 2 traps on Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.quote: Traps | Recreational License | 2

_extractedFacts.fact: Commercial anglers are limited to 25 traps on Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.quote: Commercial License | 25

_extractedFacts.fact: Recreational anglers are limited to 1 trotline with a maximum of 50 hooks on Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.quote: Trotlines - hooks must have a gap or clearance between point and shank no greater than seven sixteenths inch | Recreational License | 1 line with 50 hooks maximum

_extractedFacts.fact: Commercial anglers are limited to 3 trotlines with a maximum of 400 hooks per line on Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.quote: Commercial License | 3 lines with not more than 400 hooks on each line

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion is a private lake covering 16 acres.
_extractedFacts.quote: Lake Marion, covering 16 acres, is a Private lake situated in Unincorporated Lake County.

_extractedFacts.quote: Lake Marion, covering 16 acres, is a Private lake situated in Unincorporated Lake County.

_extractedFacts.quote: This waterbody is located within: Wekiva River Watershed

_extractedFacts.quote: Latitude | 28.95207688

_extractedFacts.quote: Longitude | -81.44551752

_extractedFacts.quote: Lake Region | Ocala Scrub (Region 7509)

_extractedFacts.fact: At a stage of 77.5 feet, structures on the shore of Lake Marion and low-lying islands are threatened by flood water.
_extractedFacts.quote: 77.5 - Structures on the shore of Lake Marion and low lying islands are threaten by flood water.

_extractedFacts.fact: At a stage of 77.09 feet, flood water approaches roads nearest to Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.quote: 77.09 - Flood water approaches roads nearest the lake.

_extractedFacts.fact: At a stage of 77 feet, flood stage is reached and areas and beaches on Lake Marion begin to flood.
_extractedFacts.quote: 77 - Flood stage - areas and beaches on Lake Marion begin to flood.

_extractedFacts.fact: The flood stage for Lake Marion is defined as 77.00 feet.
_extractedFacts.quote: minor Flooding | 77.00 ft

_extractedFacts.fact: The gauge location for Lake Marion is at coordinates 33.4495, -80.1644.
_extractedFacts.quote: Coordinates 33.4495, -80.1644

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion is part of the Santee-Cooper lakes system.
_extractedFacts.quote: Assessment of condition, growth, contribution to fish community, and diet of striped bass, white perch, and American shad young-of-the-year in the Santee-Cooper lakes, South Carolina

_extractedFacts.fact: The Big Water site on Lake Marion is located near I-95 on the Clarendon County side.
_extractedFacts.quote: The “Big Water” site was located near I-95 on the Clarendon County side (34.5178, -80.4349)

_extractedFacts.fact: The Indian Bluff site on Lake Marion is located midway down the reservoir on the Orangeburg County side.
_extractedFacts.quote: the “Indian Bluff” site was located midway down the reservoir on the Orangeburg County side (33.4319, -80.3621).

_extractedFacts.fact: White perch and American shad were the most abundant young-of-year fish collected in Lake Marion during summer 2011.
_extractedFacts.quote: During summer 2011 white perch and American shad were the most abundant species accounting for 42% and 27% of all young-of-year fish collected.

_extractedFacts.fact: Striped bass relative abundance in Lake Marion decreased from 20% in 2009 to 6% in 2011.
_extractedFacts.quote: Striped bass relative abundance decreased each study year from a high of 20% in 2009 to only 6% during 2011.

_extractedFacts.fact: The invasive Asiatic clam Corbicula fluminea is abundant in Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.quote: Corbicula fluminea, the invasive Asiatic clam, is abundant.

_extractedFacts.fact: The benthos of Lake Marion is dominated by the Asiatic clam Corbicula fluminea and the olive mystery snail Viviparus subpurpureus.
_extractedFacts.quote: As in Upper Lake Marion, the benthos of Middle and Lower Lake Marion was dominated by the Asiatic clam Corbicula fluminea and the olive mystery snail Viviparus subpurpureus.

_extractedFacts.fact: Striped bass larvae hatching in upper Lake Marion have a better chance of survival than those hatching further upstream in the river.
_extractedFacts.quote: Larvae that hatch in upper Lake Marion have a better chance of survival than those hatching further upstream, in the river.

_extractedFacts.fact: Striped bass are present in Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.quote: Santee Cooper striped bass are essentially landlocked; they live their entire lives in the lakes and tributary rivers.

_extractedFacts.fact: Harvest regulation options for striped bass in Lake Marion include specific closed seasons and creel limits.
_extractedFacts.quote: For Lakes Marion and Moultrie, Congaree, Broad, Wateree, Santee, and Cooper Rivers: Option 1: Oct 1 – May 31: 2 stripers per day, minimum length 26”, with catch & careful release allowed. June 1 – Sept 30: Closed season. No targeted catch & release fishing allowed.

_extractedFacts.fact: The South Carolina state record largemouth bass was caught from Lake Marion in 1949 and weighed 16 lbs. 2 oz.
_extractedFacts.quote: The state record largemouth bass weighed 16 lbs. 2 oz. and was caught from Lake Marion in 1949.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion and Lake Moultrie are managed as a single unit known as Santee-Cooper.
_extractedFacts.quote: Results for lakes Marion and Moultrie are often combined and reported as Santee-Cooper because they are managed as a single unit.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion bass are of average plumpness.
_extractedFacts.quote: Bass from lakes Marion, Moultrie and Jocassee tend to be of average plumpness.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion populations were nearly 100 percent Florida bass.
_extractedFacts.quote: For example, lakes Marion and Moultrie in the coastal plain were nearly 100 percent Florida bass.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion has a 12-inch minimum size limit for largemouth bass.
_extractedFacts.quote: Exceptions to this are lakes Wylie, Marion, Moultrie and the Georgia waters of the Savannah River and its reservoirs, which have a 12- inch minimum.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion is a 44,000 ha (108,724 acre) impoundment on the Santee River.
_extractedFacts.quote: Lake Marion is a partially wooded 44,000 ha impoundment on the Santee River

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion has a maximum depth of 12 meters (39.37 feet) at the dam.
_extractedFacts.quote: maximum depth of 12 m at the dam

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion is generally shallow, with an average depth of 1-3 meters (3.28-9.84 feet).
_extractedFacts.quote: overall the reservoir is shallow (1-3 m)

_extractedFacts.fact: As of summer 2008, striped bass harvest is limited to 3 fish per day greater than 660 mm TL, with a closed season from June 1 to September 30.
_extractedFacts.quote: In summer 2008 new harvest restriction went into effect that limit striped bass harvest to 3 fish/d greater than 660 mm TL, except from June 1 – September 30 when no fish may be possessed.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion and Lake Moultrie are referred to as the Santee-Cooper lakes.
_extractedFacts.source: 2016 Fisheries Investigations in Lakes and Streams
_extractedFacts.quote: Assessment of condition, growth, contribution to fish community, and diet of striped bass, white perch, and American shad young-of-the-year in the Santee-Cooper lakes, South Carolina.

_extractedFacts.fact: The average density of fish in Lake Wateree was over four times higher than the average density reported in lakes Marion and Moultrie from 2013-2015.
_extractedFacts.source: 2016 Fisheries Investigations in Lakes and Streams
_extractedFacts.quote: The average density of fish recorded in Lake Wateree of 92,597/ha was over four times higher than the average density of 21,363/ha that Bulak (2016) reported in lakes Marion and Moultrie, 2013-2015.

_extractedFacts.fact: Young-of-the-year fishes collected from Lake Marion shoreline samples included Striped Bass, White Perch, and American Shad.
_extractedFacts.source: 2016 Fisheries Investigations in Lakes and Streams
_extractedFacts.quote: Table 1. Diet categories (in bold), and the taxa assigned to each diet category, used for analysis of diet data collected from young-of- the- year fishes in Lakes Marion and Moultrie.

_extractedFacts.fact: Benthic insects were the most important prey for White Perch and small Striped Bass in Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.source: 2016 Fisheries Investigations in Lakes and Streams
_extractedFacts.quote: The most important prey of white perch and small (≤ 86 mm TL [Total Length]) Striped Bass were benthic insects.

_extractedFacts.fact: Fish and surface insects were the most important prey of large Striped Bass and American Shad in Lake Marion, respectively.
_extractedFacts.source: 2016 Fisheries Investigations in Lakes and Streams
_extractedFacts.quote: Fish and surface insects were the most important prey of large (> 86 mm TL) Striped Bass and American Shad, respectively.

_extractedFacts.fact: There was significant biological diet overlap between White Perch and small Striped Bass in Lake Marion.
_extractedFacts.source: 2016 Fisheries Investigations in Lakes and Streams
_extractedFacts.quote: There was significant biological overlap (Horn’s index = 0.91) in the diets of White Perch and small Striped Bass indicating the potential for resource competition between these fishes depending on prey availability.

_extractedFacts.fact: Largemouth bass in Lake Marion have a minimum size limit of 14 inches.
_extractedFacts.source: South Carolina Freshwater Fish Size & Possession Limits
_extractedFacts.quote: Lakes Blalock, Greenwood, Jocassee, Marion, Monticello, Moultrie, Murray, Secession, Wateree, Wylie and the middle reach of the Saluda River and the upper reach of the Santee River | Largemouth Bass | 14 inches min | No more than 5 combined total of smallmouth, largemouth, redeye bass or their hybrids

_extractedFacts.fact: The possession limit for largemouth bass in Lake Marion is no more than 5 combined total of smallmouth, largemouth, redeye bass or their hybrids.
_extractedFacts.source: South Carolina Freshwater Fish Size & Possession Limits
_extractedFacts.quote: Lakes Blalock, Greenwood, Jocassee, Marion, Monticello, Moultrie, Murray, Secession, Wateree, Wylie and the middle reach of the Saluda River and the upper reach of the Santee River | Largemouth Bass | 14 inches min | No more than 5 combined total of smallmouth, largemouth, redeye bass or their hybrids

_extractedFacts.fact: In upper Lake Marion, largemouth bass are found in 5-8 feet of water during the day around woody and weedy cover.
_extractedFacts.source: SCDNR - News Releases - Freshwater Fishing Trends
_extractedFacts.quote: largemouth bass in upper Lake Marion will be holding in 5-8 feet of water during the day around the plentiful woody and weedy cover in this section of the lake

_extractedFacts.fact: In upper Lake Marion, crappie are found on deeper brush.
_extractedFacts.source: SCDNR - News Releases - Freshwater Fishing Trends
_extractedFacts.quote: Crappie will be on deeper brush but biting well

_extractedFacts.fact: In upper Lake Marion, bream are found in 3-7 feet of water around weed edges and cypress tree clusters.
_extractedFacts.source: SCDNR - News Releases - Freshwater Fishing Trends
_extractedFacts.quote: bream will be in 3-7 feet of water around weed edges and cypress tree clusters.

_extractedFacts.fact: July catfish patterns at the top of Santee (Lake Marion) involve fishing shallow flats less than 6 feet deep early and late, and drift-fishing deeper water along channels mid-day.
_extractedFacts.source: SCDNR - News Releases - Freshwater Fishing Trends
_extractedFacts.quote: The July catfish patterns at the top of Santee typically involve fishing shallow flats less than 6 feet deep early and late, then drift-fishing deeper water along the channels mid-day.

_extractedFacts.fact: Nocturnal catfishing on shallow flats in upper Lake Marion is productive on calm nights.
_extractedFacts.source: SCDNR - News Releases - Freshwater Fishing Trends
_extractedFacts.quote: Note that nocturnal catfishing the shallow flats from an anchored setup is very productive on calm nights.

_extractedFacts.fact: Native grasses in Lake Marion have improved bass spawning habitat and provide cover for fish during hot weather.
_extractedFacts.source: Summer Hotspots for South Carolina Fishing
_extractedFacts.quote: These grasses have helped boost the population of largemouth because they have vastly improved bass spawning habitat over the past few years. The grass also provides cover needed for attracting and holding the fish during hot weather.

_extractedFacts.fact: Largemouth bass in Lake Marion are found in heavy cover at depths of 3 to 8 feet during the summer.
_extractedFacts.source: Summer Hotspots for South Carolina Fishing
_extractedFacts.quote: There are several patterns that will produce at this time of the year and working heavy cover in water depths from 3- to 8-feet deep is a primary pattern.

_extractedFacts.fact: Largemouth bass in Lake Marion often forage on shallow flats and points where shad are present.
_extractedFacts.source: Summer Hotspots for South Carolina Fishing
_extractedFacts.quote: Even if the bass are not busting shad on the surface they will often be working shallow flats and points where shad are found.

_extractedFacts.fact: Lake Marion has a surface area of approximately 81,716 acres.
_extractedFacts.source: Spatial and temporal hydrodynamic and water quality modeling analysis of a large reservoir on the South Carolina (USA) coastal plain
_extractedFacts.quote: Lake Marion (surface area 330.7 km 2; volume 1548.3×10 6 m 3)
